53	def flush_cache(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
54	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
55	    if backend is None:
56	        return False
57	
58	    # If backend is Runtime
59	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
60	        backend = backend.endpoint
61	    return backend.flush_cache()

75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,
126	        top_p,
127	        top_k,
128	        min_p,
129	        frequency_penalty,
130	        presence_penalty,
131	        ignore_eos,

134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,

20	try:

22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196

198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

2	from dataclasses import dataclass
3	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
12	
13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...
30

32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()

56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()
165

1	"""The intermediate representation."""
2	
3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod
10	
11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg
15	
16	
17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,
137	            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
138	        }
139	
140	
141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,

3	import requests

5	
6	class TestRegexConstrainedMixin:
7	    def _run_decode_regex(
8	        self,
9	        regex,
10	        prompt,
11	        return_logprob=False,
12	        top_logprobs_num=0,
13	        n=1,
14	    ):
15	        response = requests.post(
16	            self.base_url + "/generate",
17	            json={
18	                "text": prompt,
19	                "sampling_params": {
20	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
21	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
22	                    "n": n,
23	                    "regex": regex,
24	                },
25	                "stream": False,
26	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
27	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
28	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
29	            },
30	        )
31	
32	        ret = response.json()
33	        print(json.dumps(ret, indent=2))
34	        print("=" * 100)
35	
36	        if not isinstance(ret, list):
37	            self.fail(f"Expected response to be a list, but got {type(ret)}")
38	
39	        for item in ret:
40	            text = item.get("text", "").strip()
41	            if not text:
42	                self.fail("Generated text is empty.")
43	
44	            if not self.regex_match(text, regex):
45	                self.fail(f"Text '{text}' does not match regex pattern.")
46	
47	    def regex_match(self, text, pattern):
48	        import re
49	
50	        return re.match(pattern, text) is not None
51	
52	    def test_regex_generate_email(self):
53	        pattern = r"^user@example\.com$"
54	        prompt = "Generate an email address:"
55	
56	        self._run_decode_regex(
57	            regex=pattern,
58	            prompt=prompt,
59	            n=3,
60	        )
61

63	        pattern = r"^(Hello|Hi|Hey)$"
64	        prompt = "Generate a greeting:"
65	
66	        self._run_decode_regex(
67	            regex=pattern,
68	            prompt=prompt,
69	            n=3,
70	        )
71	
72	    def test_regex_generate_number(self):
73	        pattern = r"^\d{3}$"
74	        prompt = "Generate a three-digit number:"
75	
76	        self._run_decode_regex(

223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,

291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )

294	        return run_program_batch(

316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:

322	        else:

324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]

13	)

15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20

23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

1	import json
2	
3	import requests
4	
5	
6	class TestRegexConstrainedMixin:
7	    def _run_decode_regex(
8	        self,
9	        regex,
10	        prompt,
11	        return_logprob=False,
12	        top_logprobs_num=0,
13	        n=1,
14	    ):
15	        response = requests.post(
16	            self.base_url + "/generate",
17	            json={
18	                "text": prompt,
19	                "sampling_params": {
20	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
21	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
22	                    "n": n,
23	                    "regex": regex,
24	                },
25	                "stream": False,
26	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
27	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
28	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
29	            },
30	        )
31	
32	        ret = response.json()
33	        print(json.dumps(ret, indent=2))
34	        print("=" * 100)
35	
36	        if not isinstance(ret, list):
37	            self.fail(f"Expected response to be a list, but got {type(ret)}")
38	
39	        for item in ret:
40	            text = item.get("text", "").strip()
41	            if not text:
42	                self.fail("Generated text is empty.")
43	
44	            if not self.regex_match(text, regex):
45	                self.fail(f"Text '{text}' does not match regex pattern.")
46	
47	    def regex_match(self, text, pattern):
48	        import re
49	
50	        return re.match(pattern, text) is not None
51	
52	    def test_regex_generate_email(self):
53	        pattern = r"^user@example\.com$"
54	        prompt = "Generate an email address:"
55	
56	        self._run_decode_regex(
57	            regex=pattern,
58	            prompt=prompt,
59	            n=3,
60	        )
61

63	        pattern = r"^(Hello|Hi|Hey)$"
64	        prompt = "Generate a greeting:"
65	
66	        self._run_decode_regex(
67	            regex=pattern,
68	            prompt=prompt,
69	            n=3,
70	        )
71

73	        pattern = r"^\d{3}$"

75

1	"""The intermediate representation."""
2	
3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod
10	
11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg
15	
16	
17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,

12	def _find_common_prefix(s1: str, s2: str) -> str:
13	    prefix = ""
14	    min_length = min(len(s1), len(s2))
15	    for i in range(0, min_length):
16	        if s1[i] == s2[i]:
17	            prefix += s1[i]
18	        else:
19	            break
20	    return prefix

23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise
50	
51	
52	def _is_complete_json(input_str: str) -> bool:
53	    try:
54	        orjson.loads(input_str)
55	        return True
56	    except JSONDecodeError:
57	        return False

60	def _get_tool_schema_defs(tools: List[Tool]) -> dict:
61	    """
62	    Get consolidated $defs from all tools, validating for conflicts.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found
72	    """
73	    all_defs = {}
74	    for tool in tools:
75	        if tool.function.parameters is None:
76	            continue
77	        defs = tool.function.parameters.get("$defs", {})
78	        for def_name, def_schema in defs.items():
79	            if def_name in all_defs and all_defs[def_name] != def_schema:
80	                raise ValueError(
81	                    f"Tool definition '{def_name}' has "
82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs
88	
89	
90	def _get_tool_schema(tool: Tool) -> dict:
91	    return {
92	        "properties": {
93	            "name": {"type": "string", "enum": [tool.function.name]},
94	            "parameters": (
95	                tool.function.parameters
96	                if tool.function.parameters
97	                else {"type": "object", "properties": {}}
98	            ),
99	        },
100	        "required": ["name", "parameters"],
101	    }
102	
103	
104	def infer_type_from_json_schema(schema: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
105	    """
106	    Infer the primary type of a parameter from JSON Schema.
107	
108	    Supports complex JSON Schema structures including:
109	    - Direct type field (including type arrays)
110	    - anyOf/oneOf: parameter can be any of multiple types
111	    - enum: parameter must be one of enum values
112	    - allOf: parameter must satisfy all type definitions
113	    - properties: inferred as object type
114	    - items: inferred as array type
115	
116	    Args:
117	        schema: JSON Schema definition
118	
119	    Returns:
120	        Inferred type ('string', 'number', 'object', 'array', etc.) or None
121	    """
122	    if not isinstance(schema, dict):
123	        return None
124	
125	    # Priority 1: Direct type field (including type arrays)
126	    if "type" in schema:
127	        type_value = schema["type"]
128	        if isinstance(type_value, str):
129	            return type_value
130	        elif isinstance(type_value, list) and type_value:
131	            # Handle type arrays: return first non-null type
132	            non_null_types = [t for t in type_value if t != "null"]
133	            if non_null_types:
134	                return non_null_types[0]
135	            return "string"  # If only null, default to string
136	
137	    # Priority 2: Handle anyOf/oneOf
138	    if "anyOf" in schema or "oneOf" in schema:
139	        schemas = schema.get("anyOf") or schema.get("oneOf")
140	        types = []
141	
142	        if isinstance(schemas, list):
143	            for sub_schema in schemas:
144	                inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
145	                if inferred_type:
146	                    types.append(inferred_type)
147	
148	            if types:
149	                # If all types are the same, return unified type
150	                if len(set(types)) == 1:
151	                    return types[0]
152	                # When types differ, prioritize string (safest)
153	                if "string" in types:
154	                    return "string"
155	                # Otherwise return first type
156	                return types[0]
157	
158	    # Priority 3: Handle enum (infer type from enum values)
159	    if "enum" in schema and isinstance(schema["enum"], list):
160	        if not schema["enum"]:
161	            return "string"
162	
163	        # Infer type from enum values
164	        enum_types = set()
165	        for value in schema["enum"]:
166	            if value is None:
167	                enum_types.add("null")
168	            elif isinstance(value, bool):
169	                enum_types.add("boolean")
170	            elif isinstance(value, int):
171	                enum_types.add("integer")
172	            elif isinstance(value, float):
173	                enum_types.add("number")
174	            elif isinstance(value, str):
175	                enum_types.add("string")
176	            elif isinstance(value, list):
177	                enum_types.add("array")
178	            elif isinstance(value, dict):
179	                enum_types.add("object")
180	
181	        # If type is uniform, return that type
182	        if len(enum_types) == 1:
183	            return enum_types.pop()
184	        # Mixed types, prioritize string
185	        return "string"
186	
187	    # Priority 4: Handle allOf (must satisfy all types)
188	    if "allOf" in schema and isinstance(schema["allOf"], list):
189	        schemas = schema["allOf"]
190	        for sub_schema in schemas:
191	            inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
192	            if inferred_type and inferred_type != "string":
193	                return inferred_type
194	        return "string"
195	
196	    # Priority 5: Infer object type
197	    if "properties" in schema:
198	        return "object"
199	
200	    # Priority 6: Infer array type
201	    if "items" in schema:
202	        return "array"
203	
204	    return None
205	
206	
207	def get_json_schema_constraint(
208	    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
209	) -> Optional[dict]:
210	    """
211	    Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        tool_choice: The tool choice specification
215	
216	    Returns:
217	        JSON schema dict, or None if no valid tools found
218	    """
219	
220	    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
221	        # For specific function choice, return the user's parameters schema directly
222	        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
223	        for tool in tools:
224	            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
225	                return {
226	                    "type": "array",
227	                    "minItems": 1,
228	                    "maxItems": 1,
229	                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
230	                }
231	        return None
232	    elif tool_choice == "required":
233	        json_schema = {
234	            "type": "array",
235	            "minItems": 1,
236	            "items": {
237	                "type": "object",
238	                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
239	            },
240	        }
241	        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
242	        if json_schema_defs:
243	            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
244	        return json_schema
245	
246	    return None

1	# Sampling Parameters
2	
3	This doc describes the sampling parameters of the SGLang Runtime. It is the low-level endpoint of the runtime.
4	If you want a high-level endpoint that can automatically handle chat templates, consider using the [OpenAI Compatible API](openai_api_completions.ipynb).
5	
6	## `/generate` Endpoint
7	
8	The `/generate` endpoint accepts the following parameters in JSON format. For detailed usage, see the [native API doc](native_api.ipynb). The object is defined at `io_struct.py::GenerateReqInput`. You can also read the source code to find more arguments and docs.
9	
10	| Argument                   | Type/Default                                                                 | Description                                                                                                                                                     |
11	|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
12	| text                       | `Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None`                                     | The input prompt. Can be a single prompt or a batch of prompts.                                                                                                 |
13	| input_ids                  | `Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None`                         | The token IDs for text; one can specify either text or input_ids.                                                                                               |
14	| input_embeds               | `Optional[Union[List[List[List[float]]], List[List[float]]]] = None`         | The embeddings for input_ids; one can specify either text, input_ids, or input_embeds.                                                                          |
15	| image_data                 | `Optional[Union[List[List[ImageDataItem]], List[ImageDataItem], ImageDataItem]] = None` | The image input. Supports three formats: (1) **Raw images**: PIL Image, file path, URL, or base64 string; (2) **Processor output**: Dict with `format: "processor_output"` containing HuggingFace processor outputs; (3) **Precomputed embeddings**: Dict with `format: "precomputed_embedding"` and `feature` containing pre-calculated visual embeddings. Can be a single image, list of images, or list of lists of images. See [Multimodal Input Formats](#multimodal-input-formats) for details. |
16	| audio_data                 | `Optional[Union[List[AudioDataItem], AudioDataItem]] = None`                 | The audio input. Can be a file name, URL, or base64 encoded string.                                                                                             |
17	| sampling_params            | `Optional[Union[List[Dict], Dict]] = None`                                   | The sampling parameters as described in the sections below.                                                                                                     |
18	| rid                        | `Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None`                                     | The request ID.                                                                                                                                                 |
19	| return_logprob             | `Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = None`                                   | Whether to return log probabilities for tokens.                                                                                                                 |
20	| logprob_start_len          | `Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None`                                     | If return_logprob, the start location in the prompt for returning logprobs. Default is "-1", which returns logprobs for output tokens only.                     |
21	| top_logprobs_num           | `Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None`                                     | If return_logprob, the number of top logprobs to return at each position.                                                                                       |
22	| token_ids_logprob          | `Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None`                         | If return_logprob, the token IDs to return logprob for.                                                                                                         |
23	| return_text_in_logprobs    | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to detokenize tokens in text in the returned logprobs.                                                                                                  |
24	| stream                     | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to stream output.                                                                                                                                       |
25	| lora_path                  | `Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], Optional[str]]] = None`                 | The path to the LoRA.                                                                                                                                           |
26	| custom_logit_processor     | `Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], str]] = None`                           | Custom logit processor for advanced sampling control. Must be a serialized instance of `CustomLogitProcessor` using its `to_str()` method. For usage see below. |
27	| return_hidden_states       | `Union[List[bool], bool] = False`                                            | Whether to return hidden states.                                                                                                                                |
28	| return_routed_experts      | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to return routed experts for MoE models. Requires `--enable-return-routed-experts` server flag. Returns base64-encoded int32 expert IDs as a flattened array with logical shape `[num_tokens, num_layers, top_k]`. |
29	
30	## Sampling parameters
31	
32	The object is defined at `sampling_params.py::SamplingParams`. You can also read the source code to find more arguments and docs.
33	
34	### Note on defaults
35	
36	By default, SGLang initializes several sampling parameters from the model's `generation_config.json` (when the server is launched with `--sampling-defaults model`, which is the default). To use SGLang/OpenAI constant defaults instead, start the server with `--sampling-defaults openai`. You can always override any parameter per request via `sampling_params`.
37	
38	```bash
39	# Use model-provided defaults from generation_config.json (default behavior)
40	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path <MODEL> --sampling-defaults model
41	
42	# Use SGLang/OpenAI constant defaults instead
43	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path <MODEL> --sampling-defaults openai
44	```
45	
46	### Core parameters
47	
48	| Argument        | Type/Default                                 | Description                                                                                                                                    |
49	|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
50	| max_new_tokens  | `int = 128`                                  | The maximum output length measured in tokens.                                                                                                  |
51	| stop            | `Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None`     | One or multiple [stop words](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-stop). Generation will stop if one of these words is sampled. |
52	| stop_token_ids  | `Optional[List[int]] = None`                 | Provide stop words in the form of token IDs. Generation will stop if one of these token IDs is sampled.                                        |
53	| stop_regex      | `Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None`     | Stop when hitting any of the regex patterns in this list |
54	| temperature     | `float (model default; fallback 1.0)`        | [Temperature](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-temperature) when sampling the next token. `temperature = 0` corresponds to greedy sampling, a higher temperature leads to more diversity. |
55	| top_p           | `float (model default; fallback 1.0)`        | [Top-p](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-top_p) selects tokens from the smallest sorted set whose cumulative probability exceeds `top_p`. When `top_p = 1`, this reduces to unrestricted sampling from all tokens. |
56	| top_k           | `int (model default; fallback -1)`           | [Top-k](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/how-to-get-better-outputs-from-your-large-language-model/#predictability_vs_creativity) randomly selects from the `k` highest-probability tokens. |
57	| min_p           | `float (model default; fallback 0.0)`        | [Min-p](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/issues/27670) samples from tokens with probability larger than `min_p * highest_token_probability`. |
58	
59	### Penalizers
60	
61	| Argument           | Type/Default           | Description                                                                                                                                    |
62	|--------------------|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
63	| frequency_penalty  | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of penalization grows linearly with each appearance of a token. |
64	| presence_penalty   | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens if they appeared in the generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of the penalization is constant if a token occurred. |
65	| repetition_penalty | `float = 1.0`          | Scales the logits of previously generated tokens to discourage (values > 1) or encourage (values < 1) repetition. Valid range is `[0, 2]`; `1.0` leaves probabilities unchanged. |
66	| min_new_tokens     | `int = 0`              | Forces the model to generate at least `min_new_tokens` until a stop word or EOS token is sampled. Note that this might lead to unintended behavior, for example, if the distribution is highly skewed towards these tokens. |
67	
68	### Constrained decoding
69	
70	Please refer to our dedicated guide on [constrained decoding](../advanced_features/structured_outputs.ipynb) for the following parameters.
71	
72	| Argument        | Type/Default                    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
73	|-----------------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
74	| json_schema     | `Optional[str] = None`          | JSON schema for structured outputs.                                                                                                            |
75	| regex           | `Optional[str] = None`          | Regex for structured outputs.                                                                                                                  |
76	| ebnf            | `Optional[str] = None`          | EBNF for structured outputs.                                                                                                                   |
77	| structural_tag  | `Optional[str] = None`          | The structural tag for structured outputs.                                                                                                       |
78	
79	### Other options
80	
81	| Argument                      | Type/Default                    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
82	|-------------------------------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
83	| n                             | `int = 1`                       | Specifies the number of output sequences to generate per request. (Generating multiple outputs in one request (n > 1) is discouraged; repeating the same prompts several times offers better control and efficiency.) |
84	| ignore_eos                    | `bool = False`                  | Don't stop generation when EOS token is sampled.                                                                                               |
85	| skip_special_tokens           | `bool = True`                   | Remove special tokens during decoding.                                                                                                         |
86	| spaces_between_special_tokens | `bool = True`                   | Whether or not to add spaces between special tokens during detokenization.                                                                     |
87	| no_stop_trim                  | `bool = False`                  | Don't trim stop words or EOS token from the generated text.                                                                                    |
88	| custom_params                 | `Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None` | Used when employing `CustomLogitProcessor`. For usage, see below.                                                                              |
89	
90	## Examples
91	
92	### Normal
93	
94	Launch a server:
95	
96	```bash
97	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct --port 30000
98	```
99	
100	Send a request:
101	
102	```python
103	import requests
104	
105	response = requests.post(
106	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
107	    json={
108	        "text": "The capital of France is",
109	        "sampling_params": {
110	            "temperature": 0,
111	            "max_new_tokens": 32,
112	        },
113	    },
114	)
115	print(response.json())
116	```
117	
118	Detailed example in [send request](./send_request.ipynb).
119	
120	### Streaming
121	
122	Send a request and stream the output:
123	
124	```python
125	import requests, json
126	
127	response = requests.post(
128	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
129	    json={
130	        "text": "The capital of France is",
131	        "sampling_params": {
132	            "temperature": 0,
133	            "max_new_tokens": 32,
134	        },
135	        "stream": True,
136	    },
137	    stream=True,
138	)
139	
140	prev = 0
141	for chunk in response.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
142	    chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
143	    if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
144	        if chunk == "data: [DONE]":
145	            break
146	        data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
147	        output = data["text"].strip()
148	        print(output[prev:], end="", flush=True)
149	        prev = len(output)
150	print("")
151	```
152	
153	Detailed example in [openai compatible api](openai_api_completions.ipynb).
154	
155	### Multimodal
156	
157	Launch a server:
158	
159	```bash
160	python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path lmms-lab/llava-onevision-qwen2-7b-ov
161	```
162	
163	Download an image:
164	
165	```bash
166	curl -o example_image.png -L https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/examples/assets/example_image.png?raw=true
167	```
168	
169	Send a request:
170	
171	```python
172	import requests
173	
174	response = requests.post(
175	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
176	    json={
177	        "text": "<|im_start|>system\nYou are a helpful assistant.<|im_end|>\n"
178	                "<|im_start|>user\n<image>\nDescribe this image in a very short sentence.<|im_end|>\n"
179	                "<|im_start|>assistant\n",
180	        "image_data": "example_image.png",
181	        "sampling_params": {
182	            "temperature": 0,
183	            "max_new_tokens": 32,
184	        },
185	    },
186	)
187	print(response.json())
188	```
189	
190	The `image_data` can be a file name, a URL, or a base64 encoded string. See also `python/sglang/srt/utils.py:load_image`.
191	
192	Streaming is supported in a similar manner as [above](#streaming).
193	
194	Detailed example in [OpenAI API Vision](openai_api_vision.ipynb).
195	
196	### Structured Outputs (JSON, Regex, EBNF)
197	
198	You can specify a JSON schema, regular expression or [EBNF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extended_Backus%E2%80%93Naur_form) to constrain the model output. The model output will be guaranteed to follow the given constraints. Only one constraint parameter (`json_schema`, `regex`, or `ebnf`) can be specified for a request.
199	
200	SGLang supports two grammar backends:
201	
202	- [XGrammar](https://github.com/mlc-ai/xgrammar) (default): Supports JSON schema, regular expression, and EBNF constraints.
203	  - XGrammar currently uses the [GGML BNF format](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/grammars/README.md).
204	- [Outlines](https://github.com/dottxt-ai/outlines): Supports JSON schema and regular expression constraints.
205	
206	If instead you want to initialize the Outlines backend, you can use `--grammar-backend outlines` flag:
207	
208	```bash
209	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct \
210	--port 30000 --host 0.0.0.0 --grammar-backend [xgrammar|outlines] # xgrammar or outlines (default: xgrammar)
211	```
212	
213	```python
214	import json
215	import requests
216	
217	json_schema = json.dumps({
218	    "type": "object",
219	    "properties": {
220	        "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
221	        "population": {"type": "integer"},
222	    },
223	    "required": ["name", "population"],
224	})
225	
226	# JSON (works with both Outlines and XGrammar)
227	response = requests.post(
228	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
229	    json={
230	        "text": "Here is the information of the capital of France in the JSON format.\n",
231	        "sampling_params": {
232	            "temperature": 0,
233	            "max_new_tokens": 64,
234	            "json_schema": json_schema,
235	        },
236	    },
237	)
238	print(response.json())
239	
240	# Regular expression (Outlines backend only)
241	response = requests.post(
242	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
243	    json={
244	        "text": "Paris is the capital of",
245	        "sampling_params": {
246	            "temperature": 0,
247	            "max_new_tokens": 64,
248	            "regex": "(France|England)",
249	        },
250	    },
251	)
252	print(response.json())
253	
254	# EBNF (XGrammar backend only)
255	response = requests.post(
256	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
257	    json={
258	        "text": "Write a greeting.",
259	        "sampling_params": {
260	            "temperature": 0,
261	            "max_new_tokens": 64,
262	            "ebnf": 'root ::= "Hello" | "Hi" | "Hey"',
263	        },
264	    },
265	)
266	print(response.json())
267	```
268	
269	Detailed example in [structured outputs](../advanced_features/structured_outputs.ipynb).
270	
271	### Custom logit processor
272	
273	Launch a server with `--enable-custom-logit-processor` flag on.
274	
275	```bash
276	python -m sglang.launch_server \
277	  --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct \
278	  --port 30000 \
279	  --enable-custom-logit-processor
280	```
281	
282	Define a custom logit processor that will always sample a specific token id.
283	
284	```python
285	from sglang.srt.sampling.custom_logit_processor import CustomLogitProcessor
286	
287	class DeterministicLogitProcessor(CustomLogitProcessor):
288	    """A dummy logit processor that changes the logits to always
289	    sample the given token id.
290	    """
291	
292	    def __call__(self, logits, custom_param_list):
293	        # Check that the number of logits matches the number of custom parameters
294	        assert logits.shape[0] == len(custom_param_list)
295	        key = "token_id"
296	
297	        for i, param_dict in enumerate(custom_param_list):
298	            # Mask all other tokens
299	            logits[i, :] = -float("inf")
300	            # Assign highest probability to the specified token
301	            logits[i, param_dict[key]] = 0.0
302	        return logits
303	```
304	
305	Send a request:
306	
307	```python
308	import requests
309	
310	response = requests.post(
311	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
312	    json={
313	        "text": "The capital of France is",
314	        "custom_logit_processor": DeterministicLogitProcessor().to_str(),
315	        "sampling_params": {
316	            "temperature": 0.0,
317	            "max_new_tokens": 32,
318	            "custom_params": {"token_id": 5},
319	        },
320	    },
321	)
322	print(response.json())
323	```
324	
325	Send an OpenAI chat completion request:
326	
327	```python
328	import openai
329	from sglang.utils import print_highlight
330	
331	client = openai.Client(base_url="http://127.0.0.1:30000/v1", api_key="None")
332	
333	response = client.chat.completions.create(
334	    model="meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct",
335	    messages=[
336	        {"role": "user", "content": "List 3 countries and their capitals."},
337	    ],
338	    temperature=0.0,
339	    max_tokens=32,
340	    extra_body={
341	        "custom_logit_processor": DeterministicLogitProcessor().to_str(),
342	        "custom_params": {"token_id": 5},
343	    },
344	)
345	
346	print_highlight(f"Response: {response}")
347	```

58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later

73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging

80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]

124	class CacheEntry:

126	    event: Event

128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
